us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, Threats, and Toll Talk
us-politics.body:
Trump held a news conference doubling down on a Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet U.S. demands, warning of expanded bombing if no deal is reached. He invoked God's support for the U.S. cause and suggested charging tolls for passage through the Strait of Hormuz - a waterway Iran has threatened to close. OPEC+ has already agreed to boost output once the strait reopens, signaling markets are pricing in a resolution.

Trump brushed off questions about potential war crimes from strikes on civilian infrastructure. A missing F-15 airman was rescued in Iran, a detail Trump highlighted publicly. The Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Caine, offering no explanation. Critics on the left warn the threats cross legal and moral lines; supporters argue maximum pressure is the only language Tehran understands.

sources.title: Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth

us-politics.headline: Hegseth Faces Impeachment Push and Military Education Overhaul
us-politics.body:
Democratic Rep. Ansari introduced articles of impeachment against Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, citing his conduct around the Iran conflict. The move is largely symbolic given Republican control of the House, but it keeps pressure on a Pentagon chief who has faced repeated controversy. The canceled Tuesday briefing added to questions about transparency at the Defense Department.

Separately, Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and redirected partnerships toward Hillsdale College, a conservative school with no federal funding. Supporters call it a long-overdue ideological correction in officer training. Critics argue it narrows the intellectual pipeline for military leadership and prioritizes politics over merit.

sources.title: House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale

us-politics.headline: Trump Blasts Supreme Court, Midterms Money Floods In Early
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariff cases, a rare move that drew criticism from legal observers across the spectrum who warn against executive pressure on the judiciary. He also claimed at the Easter Egg Roll that he would win the presidency of Venezuela if he learned Spanish quickly - a remark that drew laughs but also scrutiny over its diplomatic implications.

On the electoral front, a GOP super PAC announced a $342 million investment in 2026 Senate races, while an AI-backed super PAC is expanding into multiple Republican primaries after claiming early wins. In Virginia, Governor Youngkin signaled he has future political ambitions, while polls show Democratic gubernatorial candidate Abigail Spanberger losing ground amid a gerrymandering controversy.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Trump claims he’d win as the president of  Venezuela — just needs to 'quickly' learn Spanish
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Trump roasts Biden over autopen use in viral interaction with kids at White House Easter Egg Roll

us-politics.headline: Immigration, Title IX, and Methane Rules Shift Under Trump
us-politics.body:
The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity, reverting to a narrower sex-based definition. The move fulfills a campaign promise but faces likely legal challenges from civil rights groups. The EPA also took an early step toward rolling back methane flaring rules, a priority for the energy industry that environmental advocates say will worsen emissions.

On immigration, Senator Mullin pressed cities with international airports to partner with federal customs enforcement, warning of consequences for those that refuse. Arizona Republicans forced a probe of one county's anti-ICE policies. A bipartisan oddity emerged on farm labor: both conservatives and liberals are pushing back against proposed H2A visa changes, with each side citing different concerns about costs and worker protections.

sources.title: Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Mullin to cities with international airports: ‘You’ve got to partner with us’
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith Arrested, Charged With Five Afghan War Crimes
au-politics.body:
Former SAS soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith was arrested by Australian Federal Police at Sydney Airport on Tuesday and charged with five counts of murder relating to alleged war crimes in Afghanistan. The AFP commissioner confirmed details of the arrest publicly, marking a landmark moment in Australia's reckoning with alleged unlawful killings by special forces.

Roberts-Smith has consistently denied wrongdoing. He lost a defamation case in 2023 after a Federal Court found the allegations were substantially proven on the civil standard, but criminal charges require proof beyond reasonable doubt. The Australian War Memorial has also announced it will review his display. The case now moves to the criminal courts, where the burden of proof is significantly higher.

sources.title: News live: Australians buy record number of new EVs in March; Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display
Details of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest revealed by AFP commissioner - video
Afternoon Update: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested over alleged war crimes; Trump’s Iran deadline nears; and the appeal of barefoot hiking
Ben Roberts-Smith arrested by Australian federal police at Sydney airport – video
Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
Breaking: Ben Roberts-Smith charged with war crimes

au-politics.headline: Trump Claims Australia Didn't Help on Iran as Deadline Looms
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump publicly claimed Australia failed to assist the United States in its confrontation with Iran, prompting a pointed response from Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, who declined to engage directly, saying the claim was not a question for him. Trump's rhetoric on Iran has shifted repeatedly in recent days, with the president alternating between warnings and apparent de-escalation.

The comments put Albanese in a delicate position ahead of the federal election, balancing the alliance relationship against domestic pressure to avoid entanglement in US military action. Trump's Iran deadline is approaching, and the diplomatic temperature between Washington and its allies remains elevated.

sources.title: 'That's not a question for me': Albanese on Trump saying Australia 'didn't help' Iran war – video
'I'm not worried about it': Donald Trump's shifting rhetoric on the Iran war

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Drives Record EV Sales and Dangerous Panic Buying
au-politics.body:
Australia recorded its highest-ever monthly new electric vehicle sales in March, with analysts linking the spike directly to the ongoing fuel crisis and rising petrol prices. The data suggests the crisis is accelerating a consumer shift that policy incentives alone had not achieved.

On the ground, the picture is less orderly. Petrol stations reported customers stockpiling fuel in everything from soft drink bottles to 200-litre drums, prompting safety warnings from authorities. Tourist regions said the fuel shortage hit Easter long weekend visitor numbers, with some operators describing the impact as significant. The Spectator has pushed back on government messaging around fuel reporting, arguing the response has been heavy-handed.

sources.title: News live: Australians buy record number of new EVs in March; Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display
Tourist hotspots share how fuel crisis impacted long weekend visitors
New EV sales data proves fuel crisis sparks consumer shift
Coke bottles to a 200L drum: Petrol stations see dangerous fuel stockpiling
Dob in a servo? How very un-Australian

au-politics.headline: Queensland Crime Falls Per Capita; Kangaroo Island Ferry Delays Anger Locals
au-politics.body:
New Queensland government data shows crime offences per capita dropped over the past year, a finding that cuts against recent political debate over law and order in the state. Raw victim and offence numbers still concern some communities, but the per-capita trend offers a more nuanced picture than headline figures suggest.

Separately, residents and businesses on Kangaroo Island are furious after new ferry services were delayed again, with critics calling the situation pathetically inadequate. The island depends on reliable ferry links for tourism and freight. Repeated delays have compounded frustration with operator SeaLink and raised questions about government oversight of the contract.

sources.title: 'Pathetically inadequate': Anger as Kangaroo Island ferries delayed again
New data on crime in Queensland shows drop in offences per capita

world.headline: Iran War Escalates as Trump's Deal Deadline Looms
world.body:
The US-Iran conflict is intensifying on multiple fronts. Israel has struck Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant, and Iran claims a US-Israeli projectile hit a synagogue in Tehran - a strike it is using to frame the war as targeting civilians. Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in launching strikes on Israel, widening the conflict across the region. Trump is threatening further attacks on Iranian power plants while simultaneously floating the idea of charging ships for Strait of Hormuz passage.

Diplomacy looks close to dead. Little sign of a breakthrough exists as Trump's negotiating deadline nears, and analysts question why Iran would accept a ceasefire given the scale of strikes already absorbed. A rescued US airman was extracted from remote Iranian territory, a rare operational success Trump has used for domestic messaging.

sources.title: Iran says US-Israeli projectile has struck a synagogue in Tehran
Trump’s threat to strike Iran’s power plants looms
‘Why would Iran agree to a ceasefire given US and Israeli track record?’
Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak

world.headline: Israel Strikes Lebanon and Gaza as Conflicts Deepen
world.body:
Israeli forces are pressing a ground offensive in Lebanon while continuing strikes in Gaza. Renewed air strikes are pounding Lebanese territory, and analysts say the campaign is deliberately stoking sectarian divisions inside the country. At least 10 Palestinians were killed in an Israeli air strike near a school in Gaza, and soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron.

Protesters swarmed the US embassy in Tel Aviv demanding an end to the wars - a rare public display of dissent inside Israel. The dual-front military campaign is drawing sustained international criticism, with Lebanon's fragile political fabric under particular strain.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens

world.headline: Taiwan Opposition Leader Meets Xi as Cross-Strait Tensions Simmer
world.body:
Taiwan's leading opposition figure has travelled to China for an expected meeting with President Xi Jinping, a visit that cuts against the ruling government's posture in Taipei. The trip signals the opposition's willingness to pursue direct engagement with Beijing at a moment of sustained cross-strait friction.

The visit comes as the US and China remain locked in a broader rivalry over artificial intelligence. A new analysis finds China leading in some AI benchmarks while the US holds advantages in others - with neither side able to claim a decisive edge yet.

sources.title: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead

world.headline: Vance Backs Orban as Hungary Votes Sunday
world.body:
US Vice President JD Vance has arrived in Budapest to publicly support Viktor Orban ahead of Hungary's election on Sunday. The visit is an unusually direct intervention by a sitting US official in a European vote, and it underscores the Trump administration's alignment with nationalist governments across the continent.

Orban faces a tighter race than in previous cycles, with opposition forces consolidating around a single challenger. Vance's presence is intended to energise Orban's base and signal Washington's preference for the outcome.

sources.title: JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote

ai.headline: OpenAI's Internal Trust Crisis and Economic Vision Collide
ai.body:
OpenAI insiders are openly questioning Sam Altman's leadership, with sources describing a culture of distrust around the CEO as the company pushes deeper into commercial territory. The tension is notable given OpenAI's simultaneous release of a sweeping economic policy vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek - a document that reads more like a government manifesto than a product roadmap.

Meanwhile, a group of OpenAI alumni have been quietly deploying a new fund that could reach $100 million, suggesting that departures from the company are translating directly into competing capital. Iran has also threatened OpenAI's Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi, adding geopolitical pressure to an already turbulent moment for the company.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability in Real Production Tasks
ai.body:
Generalist's GEN-1 robotics model has reached 99% task reliability across jobs ranging from folding boxes to repairing vacuums, a threshold that matters because production environments have near-zero tolerance for failure. This is a meaningful jump from the demo-quality performance that has defined most general-purpose robotics to date.

The result signals that physical AI is closing the gap between lab benchmarks and factory floors. Combined with Anthropic's expanded compute partnership with Google and Broadcom, the infrastructure and capability layers of AI are both accelerating at the same time.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: AI Floods Music Charts While Consulting Startups Chase McKinsey
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, despite being neither human nor real. The chart takeover is a stress test for platforms that have no clear policy on synthetic artists, and it is happening faster than anyone anticipated.

On the enterprise side, Indian startup Rocket is pitching AI-generated strategy reports as a direct substitute for McKinsey-style consulting, at a fraction of the price. Both stories point to the same underlying dynamic: AI is not just augmenting creative and knowledge work, it is replacing the output entirely and undercutting the market in the process.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost

ai.headline: Google Launches Offline AI Dictation as Vibe Coding Backlash Grows
ai.body:
Google quietly released an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, putting privacy-conscious speech-to-text directly on device without a cloud dependency. The launch comes the same week an open-source macOS tool called Ghost Pepper offered similar hold-to-talk functionality, suggesting demand for local voice input is outpacing what mainstream apps currently offer.

Separately, a sharp essay circulating on Hacker News argues that vibe coding - writing software by describing intent to an AI rather than writing code directly - has become a cult that mistakes speed for craft. The debate is sharpening as tools like Freestyle launch sandboxed environments specifically built for coding agents, making the question of human oversight more urgent.

sources.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS
The cult of vibe coding is dogfooding run amok
Launch HN: Freestyle – Sandboxes for Coding Agents

btc.headline: Bitcoin Slides Toward $68K While ETF Inflows Hit February High
btc.body:
Bitcoin dropped toward $68,000 Monday as whale selling and weakening demand pressured prices, even as spot ETF inflows reached their highest level since February. The divergence suggests institutional buyers are stepping in through regulated products while larger on-chain holders reduce exposure.

Strategy continues to absorb nearly three times the new BTC supply entering the market, with analysts citing that accumulation as a potential catalyst toward $110,000. Meanwhile, major crypto assets held relatively steady as Trump set a Tuesday deadline for an Iran nuclear deal, with geopolitical uncertainty keeping traders cautious rather than reactive.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit highest level since February
Bitcoin drops toward $68,000 as demand weakens and whales sell
Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin, ether, solana hold steady as Trump sets Tuesday night deadline for Iran deal

btc.headline: SEC Moves Toward 'Reg Crypto' as Safe Harbor Reaches White House
btc.body:
SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency is close to releasing a dedicated crypto fundraising framework, informally dubbed 'reg crypto,' aimed at giving token issuers clearer rules for capital raises. Separately, a crypto market safe harbor proposal has landed at the White House for final review.

Together the moves signal the most concrete regulatory progress for the US crypto industry in years. The shift comes as the SEC under Atkins pivots sharply from the enforcement-first posture of his predecessor.

sources.title: Crypto market safe harbor lands at White House for review
SEC close to putting out 'reg crypto' for fundraising questions, Chair Atkins says

btc.headline: Bitcoin Miners Lose Ground to AI as Anthropic Signs Power Mega-Deal
btc.body:
Anthropic has signed a multi-gigawatt compute deal that puts AI firms in direct competition with Bitcoin miners for access to cheap electricity. Miners have long relied on low-cost power as their primary competitive advantage, and large AI infrastructure contracts threaten to bid up that resource in key markets.

The pressure compounds existing challenges from the 2024 halving. Grayscale added a different dimension to Bitcoin's long-term risk picture, arguing that quantum computing threats to the network are 'more social than technical' - meaning the harder problem is coordinating a protocol upgrade than the cryptography itself.

sources.title: Bitcoin miners face a new rival for cheap power as Anthropic signs multi-gigawatt compute deal
Bitcoin’s quantum challenges are ‘more social than technical’: Grayscale

btc.headline: Blockchain Evidence Convicts Terror Financiers; DeFi Security Gets Spotlight
btc.body:
On-chain data played a decisive role in convicting three terrorism financiers in Indonesia, with TRM Labs providing blockchain analytics as key courtroom evidence. The case is one of the clearest examples yet of crypto's traceability being used against illicit actors rather than enabling them.

On the DeFi security front, the Solana Foundation launched new security tools and an incident response network as attacks on the ecosystem continue. Separately, risk manager Chaos Labs ended its relationship with Aave, saying the decision was deliberate rather than abrupt - leaving the leading lending protocol searching for a new risk provider.

sources.title: Onchain evidence was key to convicting 3 terrorism financiers: TRM Labs
Solana Foundation looks to beef up DeFi security as attacks continue
Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries at varying distances, resting only between sets — brutally simple full-body conditioning that builds grip, core, and posture simultaneously

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, consciousness, and what makes you 'you' over time
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' pushes it into genuinely unsettling territory about whether the person who wakes up tomorrow is truly continuous with the person reading this now — it quietly rewires how you think about memory, responsibility, and self
